verbatim_note: All titles/urls from AGENT 4's OWN real WebSearch; independent; no fabrication.

candidates.notes: Two fresh searches with different wording than A3. All paper-like hits are the SIMBAL source atom (arxiv 2506.14038, 'Load Balancing Mixture of Experts with Similarity Preserving Routers') plus adjacent MoE-routing works (Self-Routing 2604.00421, Cosine-Similarity/Semantic-Anchor Routing 2509.14255, Rewiring Experts on the Fly 2510.14853, TARo token-level test-time alignment 2603.18411, Routing by Analogy 2601.02144, Layerwise Recurrent Router 2408.06793, Coupling Experts and Routers 2512.23447). None describe the FUSED niche of similarity-preserving orthogonal routing governing which fast-weight expert undergoes self-supervised test-time training per token. Search 2 explicitly notes SimBal results 'don't contain specific information about test-time training or expert hidden state learner.' No same-core-niche collision. Confirms A3.

recheck_searches.query: relational similarity router orthogonality gating self-supervised fast weight expert per-token test-time adaptation

candidates.notes: BRIDGING TEST (ML term + seismology term in each query). NO bridging paper found. Results split into two disjoint clusters: (1) seismology that APPLIES ML to earthquakes (Graph Neural Networks for rupture dynamics 1906.06250 / 2025JB031981, ANN/RF rupture-arrest prediction, off-fault-damage physics 1901.01771, FDEM off-fault modeling 1911.03468) -- these use ML as a tool for seismology, they do NOT transfer rupture/off-fault-damage physics into TTT/LLM hidden-state adaptation; (2) transformers applied TO seismic data (EQTransformer phase picking PMC7415159, seismic ground-response 14/15/6658, P-wave detection TransQuake, SeisBind 2601.14607). Search 2 explicitly states results 'don't appear to address the specific concept of using physics-inspired metaphors from fault dynamics and rupture propagation to explain transformer representational trajectories.' No paper occupies the fused niche of modeling TTT hidden-state adaptation as a rupture/off-fault-damage front. No bridging collision. Confirms A3.

candidates.notes: BRIDGING TEST (ML term + seismology term in each query). NO bridging paper found. ML-side hits are the SIMBAL source atom (2506.14038) plus adjacent MoE load-balancing/stability works (Latent Prototype Routing 2506.21328, EMoE Eigenbasis-Guided Routing 2601.12137, router z-loss instability material). Seismology-side hits are pure earthquake physics / ML-applied-to-seismology (fault-rupture ML review S0065268720300066, rupture propagation-and-arrest prediction GJI 224/3/1918, remote-triggering 2106.11616, earthquake resonance 1502.05485, free-oscillations 1010.5690) plus unrelated mechanical/Rayleigh damping. The clusters never merge. Search 1 explicitly states 'none of the sources directly connect seismic rupture velocity concepts or off-fault damage mechanics to mixture-of-experts routing algorithms' and that the seismic-metaphor MoE concept 'may not yet have a published research paper.' No paper uses off-fault-damage/rupture as a metaphor for MoE routing stability. No bridging collision. Confirms A3.
